summary: The session focused on the influence of cultural differences on healthcare attitudes in the southeastern United States and the strategies healthcare providers are implementing to address these differences. Key discussions included the importance of cultural competency, access to affordable healthcare, and the challenges faced in sustaining healthcare initiatives.
facts: The southeastern states have a diverse population with varying cultural beliefs that influence healthcare attitudes. Healthcare providers in the southeastern states are hiring a diverse workforce to ensure culturally competent care. Many healthcare providers are offering cultural competency training to their staff to better understand patients' cultural beliefs and practices. Some healthcare providers are incorporating traditional medicine and healing practices to accommodate patients' cultural preferences. States in the southeast are expanding Medicaid coverage to provide more affordable health insurance options. Some healthcare providers offer sliding fee scales based on income to make healthcare services more affordable. Telemedicine is being increasingly utilized in the southeast as a cost-effective method of delivering healthcare services. Community health centers are increasing access to affordable healthcare services for vulnerable populations in the southeastern states. Continued funding is necessary to sustain healthcare initiatives in the southeastern states. Engaging stakeholders in the development of healthcare initiatives can build ownership and accountability. Political regulations can significantly affect healthcare service delivery in the southeastern states. Resistance to change from some communities can pose challenges to implementing healthcare initiatives.
